mechanism_narrative: Monthly increases in real retail sales (RSAFS) signal robust consumer spending, which directly boosts corporate revenues and leads to positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: If RSAFS month-over-month changes have a negative rank correlation with subsequent 5-day returns over a 3-year walk-forward period, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 4.5%, the labor market tightens sufficiently to trigger expectations of Federal Reserve tightening, which lowers equity valuations over a multi-month horizon.
failure_condition: If 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of observations when UNRATE < 4.5 over a 5-year walk-forward window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A negative real federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS below consumer price inflation) indicates highly accommodative monetary policy that compresses discount rates and supports equity prices over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the rank correlation of the real rate signal (FEDFUNDS minus CPI inflation) with 60-day returns is less than 0.05 in absolute value over any 3-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: During early expansion regimes (rising GDP and low rates), rising housing starts lead the economic cycle and signal improving demand, which translates into positive long-term equity returns.
failure_condition: If 60-day returns are positive in less than 60% of observations when both HOUST is rising (mom) and trigger is active over a 5-year walk-forward, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in industrial production signal expanding manufacturing activity and robust economic output, which supports corporate earnings and stock prices over a quarter-long horizon.
failure_condition: If the monthly change in INDPRO has a rank correlation with subsequent 60-day returns below 0.03 over any 3-year period, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1 million units signal a severe housing downturn that contracts related industries and depresses overall economic activity, leading to negative equity returns over the near term.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when HOUST < 1000 over a 5-year walk-forward, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously low unemployment and high payroll growth indicate a very strong labor market that supports consumer confidence and spending, producing positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: If 20-day returns are negative in more than 40% of months when both conditions hold over a 3-year walk-forward, reject.

mechanism_narrative: In a stagflation regime with high inflation and rising interest rates, the Federal Reserve tightens policy to combat inflation, which depresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and economic slowdown fears.
failure_condition: If 60-day returns are negative in fewer than 70% of trigger-active months over a 5-year walk-forward window, reject.
